sound_type: growl + bleat colony -- deep male growls, female bleats, pup cries. Rich, textured colony soundscape. Less thunderous than sea lion roars. E=5
ecosystem: Offshore islands (breeding), open ocean (pelagic, non-breeding). Pribilof Islands primary rookery. Present in PNW waters during migration.
season: Breeding: June-August on Pribilof Islands. Non-breeding: pelagic, open ocean. Passes through PNW waters.
family: Otariidae (third and final entry -- eared seals)

coordinates.description: Northern Fur Seal. Pribilof Islands (St. Paul, St. George) primary breeding rookeries. Pelagic in North Pacific. Passes through PNW waters.

taxonomy_note.description: Third and final Otariidae. Otariidae trio closes (182-184). Marine mammal block closes (180-184). DENSEST FUR of any mammal (300,000 hairs/sq in). Nearly hunted to extinction. 1911 conservation treaty.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Otariidae: Steller Sea Lion (182), California Sea Lion (183), Northern Fur Seal (184) -- 3 of 3. Trio complete.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Callorhinus from Greek 'kalos' (beautiful) + 'rhinos' (nose) -- beautiful nose. ursinus from Latin 'ursinus' (bear-like) -- bear-like appearance.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Callorhinus -- monotypic genus. Only species: C. ursinus. The sole member of its genus.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Fourth Carnivora in SPS (after Harbor Seal 180, Steller Sea Lion 182, California Sea Lion 183).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Third and final Otariidae. Fur seal vs sea lion within the family. Different thermoregulation strategy (fur vs blubber).
taxonomy_note.fur_density: Up to 300,000 hairs per square inch. 2-3x denser than sea otter. Two layers: waterproof underfur + guard hairs.
taxonomy_note.marine_mammal_block: Phocidae (180-181) + Otariidae (182-184) = 5 pinnipeds, 2 families. Complete marine mammal sequence.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 100-4000 Hz across all call types (male growl, female bleat, pup cry).
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Male growl: ~100-600 Hz. Female bleat: ~300-1500 Hz. Pup cry: ~500-2000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Male growl: 1-3 seconds. Female bleat: 0.3-1.0 seconds. Pup cry: 0.2-0.5 seconds.
song_metrics.note_structure: Deep male growl (territorial), higher female bleat (recognition), thin pup cry (contact). Colony mix creates textured soundscape.
song_metrics.note_types: Male territorial growl, female recognition bleat, pup contact cry, colony cacophony.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Colony: overlapping vocalizations from all age/sex classes. Rich texture rather than monolithic roar.
song_metrics.timbre: Growling (males), bleating (females), crying (pups). More varied than sea lion colony. Textured.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Moderate colony sound. Rich texture. Less raw volume than sea lion colonies (E=6).
song_metrics.tempo: Variable by call type. Male growls sustained. Female bleats rhythmic. Pup cries rapid.
song_metrics.context: Breeding rookeries (Pribilof Islands). Territorial defense, pup recognition, social.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Individual vocal signatures for mother-pup recognition.
song_metrics.key_property: COLONY GROWL/BLEAT. DENSEST FUR (300K/sq in). Third/final Otariidae. Pelagic. Conservation story. E=5. v1.49.325.

pelage.overall: Dense, dark brown to black (wet). Lighter when dry. Males darker with thick mane. Females lighter, grayish-brown.
pelage.fur_density: Up to 300,000 hairs per square inch. Densest fur of any mammal. Two layers: waterproof underfur + coarse guard hairs.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Dense fur (obvious). External ear flaps. Smaller than sea lions. Males have thick neck mane. Long fore flippers.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Annual molt. Fur density maintained year-round. Grooming essential.
pelage.sexual_dimorphism: Males: 2.1m, up to 270 kg. Females: 1.4m, up to 50 kg. ~5:1 weight ratio. Extreme dimorphism.

physical_description.overall: Males: length 2.1m, weight up to 270 kg. Females: 1.4m, up to 50 kg. Dense fur. External ear flaps. Long fore flippers.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Dense, luxurious fur. Smaller than sea lions. External ear flaps. Long fore flippers relative to body. Males with thick neck mane.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-sized eared seal, smaller than sea lions. Very dense, dark fur. Long fore flippers. Rarely seen on shore in PNW. Pelagic offshore.

nodes.title: Densest Fur -- 300,000 Hairs per Square Inch
nodes.description: Densest fur of any mammal. Two-layer structure: waterproof underfur + guard hairs. Insulation without blubber. The adaptation that nearly caused extinction.

nodes.title: Conservation -- 1911 Treaty
nodes.description: North Pacific Fur Seal Convention (1911): first international pinniped conservation treaty. Millions to 300K. Partial recovery. Currently Vulnerable (IUCN) / Depleted (MMPA).

nodes.title: Pelagic Lifestyle -- Months at Sea
nodes.description: Open ocean lifestyle outside breeding. Rarely on land. Pribilof Islands breeding. PNW waters during migration. Unusual among pinnipeds.

nodes.title: Otariidae Trio Closes (182-184)
nodes.description: Three eared seals: giant (Steller), performer (California), specialist (Northern Fur). Marine mammal block (180-184) complete. 5 pinnipeds, 2 families.

cross_references.relationship: OTARIIDAE TRIO! Giant (Steller) vs specialist (Fur Seal). Sea lion vs fur seal. Size vs adaptation. Both eared seals.
OTARIIDAE TRIO! Performer (California) vs pelagic specialist (Northern). Docks vs open ocean. Familiar vs elusive.
MARINE MAMMAL BLOCK! Phocidae (180-181) + Otariidae (182-184) = 5 pinnipeds, 2 families. Complete sequence.
SUBSTANCE! Scene sustainer + densest fur. What endures beneath the surface. The quiet essential adaptation.
